title: lindsaea linearis
intro: Lindsaea linearis, also known as the linear maidenhair fern, is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Lindsaeaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Australia. Traditionally, Lindsaea linearis has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are said to aid in treating respiratory issues and wounds. The plant can be prepared as a tea or a tincture, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting the immune system.
